evals.expected_output: The response should identify: (1) Switch Statements / long conditional on item type — suggest Replace Conditional with Polymorphism or Strategy pattern for discount calculation; (2) Long Method — suggest Extract Method for email, DB, discount logic; (3) Large Class / violation of Single Responsibility — the method does pricing, shipping, emailing, and DB saving; (4) Hardcoded credentials (pragmatic note). Should name smells using Refactoring Guru vocabulary and suggest concrete refactoring steps.

assertions.description: Uses 'Switch Statements' smell name or equivalent
assertions.check: response mentions 'Switch Statements' or 'conditional' or 'polymorphism' in relation to item-type chain

assertions.description: Identifies Long Method smell by name

assertions.check: response mentions single responsibility or multiple concerns (pricing, email, database)

assertions.description: Recommends at least one named refactoring technique
assertions.check: response names at least one specific refactoring technique from Refactoring Guru
response uses at least 2 canonical Refactoring Guru terms

assertions.description: Mentions Strategy pattern for the discount logic
assertions.check: response mentions Strategy pattern in context of discount/type-based logic

evals.prompt: I'm building a document export system. Users can export documents as PDF, HTML, or Markdown. The export format might grow (we're thinking about adding DOCX and EPUB in the future). Each format needs different logic for headers, body text, images, and tables. What design pattern should I use and how would I structure it?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend Strategy pattern (interchangeable algorithms for the same task) or possibly Visitor (if the document tree is fixed but operations vary), explaining the trade-offs. Should explain why Strategy fits: export format is the varying algorithm, document is the context. Should sketch the structure — an Exporter interface with format-specific implementations — and note that new formats are added by creating new classes, not modifying existing ones (Open/Closed Principle). Should also mention Template Method as an alternative if the export steps are mostly the same with format-specific variations.

Should identify: (1) Shotgun Surgery — adding a channel touches every method; (2) Divergent Change — method changes when rules change AND when channels change; (3) Feature Envy — UserService is doing notification work. Should recommend Observer pattern: UserService emits events (EmailChanged, PasswordChanged, AccountDeactivated), and notification channels are subscribers. Should sketch the refactored structure and explain that adding a new channel just means registering a new subscriber. May also note Extract Class for notification logic and Move Method as stepping stones.
